name: POST /api/v1/announcement/{id}/update/status/ - unauthorized returns 401
description: Purpose: Verify that request without token returns 401 Unauthorized
descriptionHtml: Purpose: Verify that request without token returns 401 Unauthorized
statusMessage:
AssertionError: Expected: 401, Got: 404 | Page not found at /api/v1/announcement/1/update/status/

Page not found (404)

Request Method: POST
Request URL: http://api-v2-dev.joymee.uz/api/v1/announcement/1/update/status/

Using the URLconf defined in config.urls, Django tried these URL patterns, in this order:

  1. admin/
  2. api/core/
  3. api/ v1/auth/
  4. api/ v1/account/
  5. api/ v1/notification/
  6. api/ v1/chat/
  7. api/ v1/announcement/ [name='list-create']
  8. api/ v1/announcement/ <int:pk>/ [name='detail-update-delete']
  9. api/ v1/announcement/ map/ [name='map']
  10. api/ v1/announcement/ map/mini/ [name='map-mini']
  11. api/ v1/announcement/ <int:pk>/status/ [name='status-update']
  12. api/ v1/announcement/ <int:pk>/similar/ [name='similar']
  13. api/ v1/announcement/ vip/ [name='vip']
  14. api/ v1/announcement/ report/
  15. api/ v1/announcement/ comment/
  16. api/ v1/announcement/ like/
  17. api/ v1/dictionary/
  18. api/ v1/billing/
  19. swagger/ [name='schema-swagger-ui']

The current path, api/v1/announcement/1/update/status/, didn’t match any of these.

You’re seeing this error because you have DEBUG = True in your Django settings file. Change that to False, and Django will display a standard 404 page.

assert 404 == 401 + where 404 = .status_code
